objective: Camera-and-analysis-education organic Reel that both attracts top-of-funnel viewers and reduces bad-upload support load by teaching the real camera-setup requirements.
audience: Pitchers, parents, and coaches broadly interested in training technology and video analysis methods — top-of-funnel awareness.
hook: Most pitchers record from the wrong angle.
coreInsight: A front-angle, too-close, normal-speed recording produces footage that's nearly unusable for mechanics analysis; a 15-foot side view at 240 FPS SLO-MO fixes it completely.
openLoop: The hook names a common mistake — the viewer watches to confirm whether their own setup matches it.
payoff: The corrected setup and the resulting clean slow-motion footage prove the fix works, then the exact numbers are given so the viewer can copy it.

scenes.cameraDirection: Staged wrong setup: handheld phone, front-facing angle, too close, hard cut in.
scenes.soundDesign: Single low tick on the cut in.

assetReferences.description: Staged wrong-setup demo clip: handheld, front angle, too close

scenes.cameraDirection: Same staged clip continues at normal (non-slow-motion) speed — motion blur, cropped body, hard to read.
scenes.voiceOver: Too close, wrong angle, normal speed — this tells us almost nothing.
scenes.soundDesign: Slight distortion/muffled accent to sonically mark "this doesn't work."

assetReferences.description: Same staged wrong-setup clip, normal speed

scenes.cameraDirection: Hard cut to black-to-navy wipe with caption only, no footage.
Real footage of a tripod being placed at the correct side-view position.

assetReferences.description: Hands placing tripod at correct side-view position

scenes.cameraDirection: Close-up of a tape measure/marker confirming 15 feet, then tilt to phone height marker at 6 feet.
scenes.voiceOver: Fifteen feet away. Side view. Six feet high.

scenes.cameraDirection: Same pitcher, correctly shot side-view slow-motion footage, freeze at foot strike — crisp, full body visible.
scenes.voiceOver: Same pitcher. Now you can actually see the delivery.

assetReferences.description: Correctly-shot side-view slow-motion pitch, same subject

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen insert: iPhone Settings → Camera → Record Slo-mo → 240 FPS, using public/pitch-nav-slow-motion-guide.png as the reference graphic.
scenes.voiceOver: SLO-MO mode. Two hundred forty frames per second preferred, one twenty accepted.

scenes.cameraDirection: Static end card: wordmark small at top, CTA centered, requirements recap listed small beneath.
scenes.voiceOver: Get the setup right, then start your Pitch Nav analysis.

voiceOverScript: Too close, wrong angle, normal speed — this tells us almost nothing. Here's the fix. Fifteen feet away. Side view. Six feet high. Same pitcher. Now you can actually see the delivery. SLO-MO mode. Two hundred forty frames per second preferred, one twenty accepted. Get the setup right, then start your Pitch Nav analysis.
bRollInstructions: The 'wrong setup' clip can be staged (clearly a setup demonstration, not a real pitcher's analysis). The 'correct setup' clip must be the same pitcher, matched as closely as possible to the wrong-setup clip's pitch, to make the before/after honest.
editingNotes: Roughly matches content/video-templates/timelines/20s.md stretched to 24s to fit the extra measurement beat — still changes visually every 1-3 seconds. No product screen recording in this Reel; it's pre-purchase education, not a feature demo, so the CTA end card carries the product connection alone.
soundDesign: Brisk, slightly playful staccato bed — educational energy, not tense. Muffled/distorted accent on the 'wrong' footage, clean accents on the 'right' footage, to sonically reinforce the contrast.

assetReferences.description: Staged wrong-setup clip + correctly-shot same-subject clip
Brisk, slightly playful staccato bed — educational energy
